status_notes: Both documents have official English translations freely published by the FSA itself, no paywall. PDF text extraction failed on this pass (binary/compressed streams) but the documents are confirmed to exist and be linked directly from FSA's own pages.
notes: See also registry/regulation/jp/go/fsa.json (Payment Services Act) for the regulation-type file under this same namespace.

match_nodes.description: Guidelines on Cybersecurity for the Financial Sector — core operational cybersecurity control catalogue for Japanese financial institutions

data.official_name: Guidelines on Cybersecurity for the Financial Sector
data.notes: Effective October 4, 2024, following public comment (June-July 2024) and coordinated with 16 revised sector-specific supervisory guidelines. Three sections: (1) Fundamental Principles, (2) Cybersecurity Management Posture (governance, identification, protection/defense, detection, response, recovery, third-party risk — NIST-CSF-like framing), (3) Cooperation between FSA and related organizations. Widely reported (via secondary sources, not independently verified against primary text) to contain ~176 discrete response items, each split into 'Fundamental/Basic response measures' vs. 'Recommended/Desirable response measures'. The core operational cybersecurity control catalogue for all Japanese financial institutions supervised by the FSA — banks, insurers, securities firms.

urls.note: Official FSA English translation. Freely accessible; PDF text extraction failed in this pass.

match_nodes.description: Comprehensive Guidelines for Supervision of Major Banks, etc. — banking supervisory rulebook including cyber-incident reporting

data.official_name: Comprehensive Guidelines for the Supervision of Major Banks, etc.
data.notes: First English preliminary-version translation published June 8, 2022; the Japanese original remains the only legally authoritative text. Content since amended (further amendments finalized October 2024 alongside the Cybersecurity Guidelines for the Financial Sector). A large multi-chapter bank-supervision manual; the cybersecurity-specific content sits in a dedicated subsection reported as III-3-7 'Cybersecurity', covering requirements to designate a cybersecurity-responsible manager, establish an incident-response/emergency organization, maintain multi-layered defenses, conduct periodic security assessments, and report cyber incidents to the FSA immediately upon awareness.

urls.note: FSA-labeled 'preliminary version' English translation. Freely accessible; PDF text extraction failed in this pass.
